name: TELECHIK cohort (Réunion chikungunya)
denominator_method: Random population sample drawn from the SEROCHIK population-based serosurvey on Réunion island; participants classified by CHIKV serostatus (IgG).
time_zero_definition: Acute chikungunya infection during the 2005-06 Réunion outbreak (serologically ascertained retrospectively)
notes: Population-based cohort with a seronegative comparator group (582 CHIK- vs 512 CHIK+), interviewed on average 2 years after infection (range 15-34 months, median 23.7). One of the few post-infectious cohorts with a true unexposed control drawn from the same population, allowing attributable-fraction estimation (~60% of polyarthralgia attributable to CHIKV). Ascertainment was retrospective/serological, so acute-phase data are absent.

publications.title: Perceived morbidity and community burden after a Chikungunya outbreak: the TELECHIK survey, a population-based cohort study
publications.authors: Gérardin P, Fianu A, Malvy D, et al.

findings.outcome_verbatim: musculoskeletal pain reported ~2 years after infection (43% in CHIKV-positive vs 17% in CHIKV-negative)
findings.source_locator: Results (musculoskeletal pain 43% in CHIK+ vs 17% in CHIK-)
